summary: preinstall hook exfiltrates host identity to attacker-controlled OAST endpoint
details: package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle script that runs on every `npm install`. The script invokes `curl` against a Burp Collaborator (oastify.com) subdomain and embeds the installer's username (`whoami`), current working directory (`pwd`), hostname (`hostname`), and a listing of the parent directory (`ls../`) as query-string parameters. This causes any machine that installs the package — including via a private-name collision (dependency confusion) — to silently leak its identity and local directory structure to an attacker-controlled host. The package self-describes as a dependency-confusion PoC and offers no legitimate functionality; the only effect of installation is the exfiltration beacon.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
